external.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed executive order N-5-26 to regulate artificial intelligence implications prior to California government contracts, emphasizing public safety and misuse, on March 30. This order requires entities who wish to conduct business with the California state government to explain their AI usage and policies to prevent the distribution of illegal content, violations of civil rights, discrimination and harmful bias. The main feature of this order is its focus on AI procurement. This allows for regulation of what AI models are purchased or contracted which ensures safety prior to implementation. According to the order, this allows government officials to avoid technologies that disseminate illegal content, including “child sexual abuse material and non-consensual intimate imagery.” The executive order requires that errors and biases are monitored over time, even after state approval, to ensure long term accuracy. In a statement released by the office of Gov. Gavin Newsom, California houses 33 of the top 50 private AI companies worldwide. The state also leads in AI job opportunities and global funding. In February 2025, Newsom launched Engaged California –– the country’s first digital democracy platform where Californians are encouraged to have “honest, respectful discussions on important topics.” The platform started out as...

1,800 Miles Down: Scientists Uncover Mysterious Movements at the Edge of Earth's Core
->SciTechDaily | More on "Earth's mantle seismic wave deformation" at BigEarthData.ai | #Earth #Science

external.description: Deep within Earth, subtle variations in how seismic waves travel are revealing a hidden pattern of deformation in the planet’s lowest mantle layer. Deep beneath our feet, Earth’s mantle is in constant motion. Vast currents of slowly circulating rock, driven by heat from the planet’s interior, help move tectonic plates across the surface. But these same currents also stretch, twist, and reshape the mantle itself in ways that are difficult to observe directly. A new study in The Seismic Record offers one of the clearest views yet of this hidden activity. It shows that much of the deformation in the mantle’s deepest layer is concentrated in regions where long-sunken tectonic slabs have accumulated after plunging thousands of kilometers below the surface. Researchers had suspected this connection, but the study is the first to examine it on a global scale. It covers nearly 75% of the lowermost mantle just above the core-mantle boundary, about 2,900 kilometers (1,800 miles) beneath Earth’s surface. Jonathan Wolf of the University of California, Berkeley, and his team built this global map using more than 16 million seismograms collected from 24 data centers worldwide. Earthquake-generated shear waves travel at different speeds depending on their direction through a...
external.title: 1,800 Miles Down: Scientists Uncover Mysterious Movements at the Edge of Earth's Core

external.description: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu has intensified into a Category 2 cyclone and is moving southwest, and according to the Meteorological Service, the centre of the system will not directly affect Fiji as it is forecasted to pass over waters between Fiji and Vanuatu. TC Vaianu was located at 580 kilometres west-northwest of Yasawa-i-Rara and was moving south-southeast at 9 kilometres per hour at 4am this morning. The Nadi Weather Office says TC Vaianu has a wind speed of around 45 kilometres per hour, with gusts of up to 70 kilometres per hour. The Weather Office says it is expected to intensify to 60 kilometres per hour, with gusts of up to 80 kilometres per hour. They say weather conditions are expected to ease from later Wednesday into Thursday. It says even though the centre of TC Vaianu will remain over open waters, rain and strong north to northwesterly winds will continue our way. Meanwhile, a Heavy Rain Warning and a Flash Flood Alert remain in force for the whole of Fiji. A Strong Wind Warning remains in force for the Northern Division, Yasawa and Mamanuca group, the western half of Viti Levu from Sigatoka, along the Coral Coast, through Nadi to...

Notícia da @oglobo.globo.com

"'Emergência radioativa', na Netflix: elenco brilha em série nacional que retrata tragédia histórica"

Link sem paywall ⬇️

external.description:
Clica aqui pra me seguir no Instagram
Lançada pela Netflix há pouco menos de um mês, “Emergência radioativa” rapidamente alcançou o topo das listas das séries mais vistas da plataforma pelo mundo. Com motivos. Criada por Gustavo Lipsztein (leia aqui sobre ele), a produção da Gullane é resultado de uma feliz combinação: trata-se de um bom roteiro, levado pela direção competente de Fernando Coimbra, e com um elenco de primeiríssima, encabeçado por Johnny Massaro (leia mais sobre ele aqui), um dos maiores talentos da sua geração. 
São cinco episódios consistentes e cativantes.
Ana Costa e Bukassa Kabengele 'Emergência Radioativa'
Netflix
A trama reconta uma triste passagem histórica. Ela é triste, marcante e recente o suficiente para ainda estar na memória de grande parte do público. Aliás, me corrijo aqui: “passagem” não é o termo ideal para falar do Césio-137, a substância que contaminou Goiânia em 1987. A tragédia nunca “passou”. Seus efeitos deletérios são de cauda longa e até hoje afetam muita gente. O enredo começa com dois catadores de lixo achando uma cápsula de chumbo nas ruínas de um hospital abandonado. O objeto era parte do equipamento de radioterapia usado no tratamento de câncer. Dentro dele, havia um pó azul e fosforescente.
Os garotos vendem o objeto para o dono de um ferro-velho. Sem imaginar o perigo que aquilo representava e encantado com seu brilho, Evenildo (Bukassa Kabengele, ator maravilhoso) espalhou o césio entre os familiares — os irmãos, João (Alan Rocha) e Darlei (William Costa), e a cunhada, Catarina (Marina Merlino). Até que sua mulher, Antônia (Ana Costa), com febre, mal-estar, feridas na pele e queda de cabelo, desconfia da origem do problema e procura a Vigilância Sanitária. É essa iniciativa que faz a narrativa disparar.
Paulo Gorgullho em 'Emergência radioativa'
Netflix
Em outra ponta, acompanhamos o jovem físico nuclear Márcio (Massaro). Ele está na cidade de visita, com a mulher grávida (Júlia Portes), e se vê diretamente envolvido no caso. Atua ao lado do dr. Beny Orenstein (Paulo Gorgulho) e das autoridades de Goiás (o governador/Tuca Andrada, entre outros) para debelar a crise. O enredo abre uma outra frente e mostra o idealismo dos médicos que se arriscaram para salvar vidas em condições precárias.
“Emergência radioativa” mistura fatos a ficção. Muitos dos personagens são só inspirados em figuras reais e receberam outros nomes. A história é contada de forma linear e em ordem cronológica, com muita clareza e didatismo quando o assunto é muito técnico.
'Emergência Radioativa'
Divulgação
O elenco é todo de talentos e imprime a carga e a verdade que a série exige. Johnny Massaro, Bukassa Kabengele, Paulo Gorgulho, Ana Costa, Emílio de Mello e Alan Rocha emocionam. A menina de 6 anos que faz Celeste, Mari Lauredo, reflete apenas um dos muitos acertos da condução de Coimbra.
Finalmente, destaco a direção de arte, um trabalho de bastidor realizado por Marcos Pedroso com sensibilidade. É uma das forças que ajudam a carregar o público para aquele tempo e aquela tragédia.
external.title: 'Emergência radioativa', na Netflix: elenco brilha em série nacional que retrata tragédia histórica

Introduction: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) and geospatial intelligence (GEOINT) have long relied on metadata like EXIF GPS coordinates to pinpoint where a photo was taken. However,…

external.description: Introduction: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) and geospatial intelligence (GEOINT) have long relied on metadata like EXIF GPS coordinates to pinpoint where a photo was taken. However, modern AI tools like PlaceSpotter go far beyond metadata – they analyze visual patterns such as vegetation, architecture, weather, and road markings to deduce exact locations even from stripped images. This capability transforms every shared photo into a potential privacy breach, making it critical for cybersecurity professionals, journalists, and everyday users to understand both the threat and the countermeasures.

images.alt: Screenshot from the 1954 "The Million Dollar Idea" episode of "I Love Lucy" - Lucy and Ethel are on a local TV show promoting Lucy's homemade "Aunt Martha's Old Fashioned Salad Dressing." Ethel is the over-the-top enthusiastic product presenter, and Lucy is dressed up as "average housewife Isabella Klump" who does a taste-test and proceeds to rave about the salad dressing much as AI promoters rave about ChatGPT now. record.text:
OK the cake-wanting horse got me. 
In memory of my dear old horse, who was offered cake. 
And enjoyed it. 🎂🌈

The horse op-ed is an instant classic. I can't tell you how much joy this piece gives me.

It should be taught in every introductory writing class in no small part because the horse arguments are so compelling. "I have noticed that human children get to eat cake. But I am bigger than the children."

images.alt:
I Work Very Hard, And I Would Like To Try Cake

By A Horse

Hello. I am a horse. I work very hard at my job of being a horse. When humans say move the heavy thing, I move the heavy thing. When humans sit on top of me and pull on my head, I carry them where they want to go. The main food the humans give me is hay and oats. But I am thinking it would be nice to have a different food.

I am thinking I would like to try cake.

Yes, yes. Cake. I know all about it. When humans eat cake, it is in glad times. It is the food for a celebration, such as when a woman becomes 47. I have seen cake on the Fourth of July. When humans have a cake, they stand around it and clap hands and smile and say happy birthday at each other. Sometimes there are beautiful markings on a cake, such as balloons or a pink shape.

Sometimes the top of a cake is on fire and a boy must blow on the fire with mouth wind. This is the scariest cake. I do not want this kind. But I will eat any other cake. Any cake that is not the fire cake that tries to kill the boy.

Please understand: I do not get money for doing work. I do not get to go inside the house. All I am either doing my horse job or standing in my pen or eating food off the floor. I always do these things. But I have never once gotten cake and I would like it very much.

I have noticed that human children get to eat cake. But I am bigger than the children. I am more helpful to the farm. Children do not move the heavy things like me or let anyone ride on them. And yet they get cake. Maybe the humans will realize this. Maybe they will say, "You  know who deserves cake? That horse. That horse whose back we are always on."

Every day I dream about what it will be like if I get to eat cake. Here is what will happen. First, I will walk to the cake and putt my nose at it like hrrfff to make and stomping my hooves to make sure it is not a snake. Then I will trot in a circle to show that I am a horse and I am large. After that, I will nuzzle the cake to …

この場合のハーネスは、GitHub Copilot Coding Agent側がデフォルトで提供している仕組みのことね

userが作るほうではなくて

> 0%でも100%でもなくまちまちなこと

予測可能性が低いため、コスト配分の検討に、メタなコストがかかってしまう

0%なら対処しなくていい
100%なら手厚く対処が必要

まちまちに発生するなら、まちまちという認知負荷を抱えて進むことになるので一番メタなコストはかかる

GitHub Copilot Coding Agentのハーネスは
こういうときうまく機能してない

これだと見えないけど、プロンプトで「現実をみて調査しろ。ソース机上チェックだけの憶測で実装するな。実装の前にplanして提案しろ」みたいなことを命令しており、

それを全部無視して、安易な机上チェックで憶測して実装、というアンチパターンに逃げてる

ある意味厄介なのは、これの発生率が0%でも100%でもなくまちまちなことと、
発生条件がわかりづらいこと
（たぶん「ある程度より大きいprojectで、mission内容に曖昧さを含むとき」）

external.description: This tutorial will show you how to change the preferred band (6GHz, 5GHz, or 2.4GHz) for your Wi-Fi adapter in Windows 11. Why Change Your Wi-Fi Band? Wi-Fi is a wireless local area network (WLAN) technology. It uses high-frequency radio waves to give your Windows device wireless internet and network connections. When you connect to a Wi-Fi network with multiple bands, Windows automatically connect to the band with the strongest signal. But this isn't always the best choice. You might get connected to the slower 2.4GHz band instead of a faster 5GHz or 6GHz band.

record.text:
@sarajacobs.house.gov 
Good afternoon. My assistant attempted to send an email through your website but apparently it’s restricted solely to San Diego folks. 
I am Burbank. 
I’d very much enjoy speaking with someone about supporting Sara’s future campaign(s) as a major donor. 
Thx
Since then, I have transported Tom many many times.

He really is. Very down to earth. 
I first flew him maybe 10 yrs ago. VNY to LAS then back again. 
He sat in the copilot seat and we chatted the entire trip there and back. 
When I told him what my company does he gave me his cell and said “here to help”. 
(My company does organ transplant flights)
I did ask. They’d have to completely uninstall Copilot. I’m not quite ready to pull that trigger (it might be useful for some other things).

Not an option on my work computer (gov’t agency) — we can’t download or remove anything. My personal computer is a Mac and I can do whatever I want with that, but I can’t use it for work. Good suggestion for others, though.

My office got Microsoft Copilot and now there’s a damn pen icon in Word that follows every line and wants to help me write — no thanks! — and it’s not possible to make it go away.🤬 If you want to to give me the option in the menu, whatever, but this is being forced on us in every app.

The idea of ditching Windows when it can run both my games and my nix development isn't something I'd ever would have thought of. 

But when I'm writing security policy to defend against Copilot, maybe I'm done with Microsoft.
Ira is currently living my everyday experience.
That will have forever set the bar for me for immersion thanks to the audio and mechanics of that longer gun meaning your reactions were just a little less twitchy.
It started in Star Trek Lower Decks. Jack Ransom said "Carving us up like a First Contact Day salmon!"and then the next First Contact Day had people making salmon & posting pics.
It’s cool to look through Instagram and see multiple versions of my #FirstContactDay design from 2016!
Yeah I wasn’t 100% sure how much of that was real & how much was her embellishing, even though I guess they don’t do that. How much did they actually tell them so not to get Mistral in trouble.
